channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Large deep dangerous pot hole next to the speed bump, 9th March
item.description:
There is a large pot hole next to the speed bum , damages cars have reported before months ago and still not fixed
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 113, Abbey Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pot hole approx 60- 70 cm, 9th March
item.description:
This has been here for a few months after some roadworks a large porthole was left
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 47, Adys Road, Greater London

item.title: Large pot hole approx 60- 70 cm, 9th March
item.description:
This has been here for a few months after some roadworks a large porthole was left
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 47, Adys Road, Greater London

item.title: Huge area of potholes, 9th March
item.description:
Kemsing road junction with Woolwich Road. Huge area of potholes on west carriageway. Cars pull out into oncoming traffic to avoid. Please fix.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Huge potholes at angerstein roundabout, 9th March
item.description:
Huge pothole at the angerstein roundabout as you turn west to head towards Greenwich along Trafalgar Road.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Willowbrook Road, Peckham, Greater London

item.title: 25 cm pot hole, 9th March
item.description:
This pot hole has been there for a few weeks and getting worse
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Willowbrook Road, Peckham, Greater London

item.title: Opposite side road to 45 Dartmouth Road, 9th March
item.description:
Pavement on opposite soad of road to 45 Dartmouth Road, NW2, is in disrepair and has been for at least 3 years. Severe tripping hazard as paving is uneven. Parts of broken paving slabs physically move when stepped on further adding to this.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mapesbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped sofa and waste, 9th March
item.description:
This was dropped here today

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Stoke Newington High Street, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Driver of vehicle littering Woolwich road, 9th March
item.description:
I followed this vehicle along Woolwich Road at exactly 17:29 today, 9th March, as the driver threw several pieces of litter out of his window into the road, including a large fast food fried chicken packaging and papers. This was disgusting and inappropriate, and the driver should be fined for littering.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Someone has dumped all their food waste in our general waste bin, 9th March
item.description:
Last week someone dumped all their food waste in our general waste bin 10 Wayland Avenue. No one will collect it and it’s rotting.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Wayland Avenue, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: James Watt Way area Erith - Litter not being removed, 9th March
item.description:
McDonalds & KFC are here, together with accumulated leaves & debris from the past year. No meaningful clearing has been carried out. Please advise your staff & contractor that their job is to sweep the rubbish away. Not to just look at it !
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Colebrook Street, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubish everywhere where kids play, 9th March
item.description:
The rubbish situation in Larkhall park is so embarrassing I avoid taking visitors there, and regularly have to explain to my kids how acceptable the rubbish situation is. If we can’t afford to pay someone to keep this clean can we at least install some rubbish picking equipment so citizens can take action to stop this area continuously getting overrun with rubbish? It’s sad that our children are growing up in this third world environment.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Courland Grove, Stockwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The early repot is now even worse, 9th March
item.description:
Because the amount of dog waste in front of 3 Browning Street was so substantial, it's now spread on the pavement, and it's right in front of our doorstep and the lower ground windows to flat 2. it was reported by my number but is still there.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Colworth Grove, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Powell Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Graffiti on wall opposite sue godfrey nature reserve, 9th March
item.description:
Graffiti present for ~ 3 months

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Berthon Street, Deptford
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: URGENT: Bus Route Carriageway Failure Causing Structural Vibration to Homes on Radbourne Road, 9th March
item.description:
The carriageway on Radbourne Road has deteriorated into multiple deep potholes and extensive cracking directly within the heavy vehicle wheel track of a bus route. The asphalt surface has broken through exposing the underlying aggregate and previous patch repairs have failed, indicating likely structural failure of the carriageway rather than isolated potholes. These defects appear to follow previous rectangular reinstatement areas, suggesting settlement or failure of a utility trench repair under repeated heavy bus loading. The road surface is now significantly degraded, with multiple impact potholes forming along the same wheel path where buses consistently strike the defects. Heavy buses are impacting these potholes every 8–10 minutes, generating extremely violent shock loads that travel through the ground and into nearby residential properties. The vibration is severe enough to repeatedly wake residents during the night and is now causing internal disturbance within homes, including shaking floors, rattling doors and cupboards, and visible cracking to interior finishes. This has become an ongoing nuisance and potential structural risk to nearby properties. Given the condition of the road and the repeated heavy vehicle loading, this appears to require urgent inspection and full-depth carriageway repair rather than further temporary patching.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cambray Road, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bag containing nitrous oxide cylinders dumped behind bike cage, 9th March
item.description:
Bag containing nitrous oxide cylinders dumped behind bike cage
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Southborough Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
